close_reason: Acceptable. PartialEq/Eq on SecretUri is useful for tests and config dedup. URI normalization is already stable (no percent-encoding or case folding). Removing the derive would break more than it protects.
description: SecretUri in secretx-core/src/lib.rs derives PartialEq and Eq. This makes URI normalization behavior (case folding, trailing slashes, percent-encoding) a forever-stable public contract: any change to how URIs are parsed or normalized that changes equality results is a breaking change. For example, if the backend name is currently compared case-sensitively and that is later changed to case-insensitive, any code using SecretUri as a HashMap key or in an assert_eq\! will silently break. What goes wrong: adding PartialEq/Eq to a type that wraps a parsed URI means the parsed representation's equality semantics are part of the public API forever. If the internal representation ever changes (e.g. params stored in a sorted map vs insertion-order), equality behavior changes and callers notice. Fix: remove the derived PartialEq/Eq from SecretUri, or document explicitly what equality means (byte-for-byte identity of the original URI string vs structural equality after parsing).
title: SecretUri derives PartialEq/Eq locking normalization semantics
